recommendation: No critical/high findings are open, but repairable medium findings remain; handle them through the existing repair or revisit paths.

prepContract.purpose: Preparation contract derived from phase-task-checklist and phase-capability-checklist. It summarizes what the current phase should implement by group and which groups still look risky or unresolved.

notes: The latest test build report is accepted as PASS evidence for matching referenced tests.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/profile
requiredBehaviors: `Profile.kt` only renders the packaged profile avatar with static user text; it does not open an image viewer, decode bitmaps, inspect EXIF metadata, or support zoom/preview gestures.

`Profile.kt` renders a back command, static profile action cards, and a logout button. The back arrow calls `navController.navigateUp()`, the action cards keep placeholder click handlers, and logout clears the start destination stack before routing to onboa...

`Profile.kt` composes against `ProfileViewModel` callbacks and navigates immediately from user actions, while keeping the visible profile identity and placeholder cards static until logout is requested.
`Profile.kt` uses only packaged resources, local click handlers, and navigation callbacks; it does not request runtime permissions or access protected Android APIs.
`ProfileViewModel.onEvent` propagates the logout action by toggling login state and then invoking the navigation callback, so the profile screen reacts immediately to the logout event.
`ProfileViewModel.onEvent` only toggles persisted login state and invokes the logout callback; it does not request permissions or call protected APIs.
`ProfileViewModel` has no independent load/resume teardown path; its lifecycle responsibility is limited to servicing the logout event while the profile screen is active.
`ProfileEvent.kt` defines only the `PerformLogout` callback payload and does not represent permission requests, results, or protected API access.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests need to be covered by the latest accepted test gate or explicit verificationEvidence.
architectureHints: If several source files are merged, keep business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints.

implementationGroups.title: Verification scope: com/example
requiredBehaviors: `ExampleInstrumentedTest.kt` is only a package-name smoke test and does not verify business behavior.
`addition_isCorrect` is only a placeholder local test and does not represent real CRUD or persistence behavior in the source app.
`ExampleUnitTest.kt` is only a placeholder arithmetic test and does not verify app behavior.
riskHints: source tests should become target focused tests or explicit verification evidence
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests need to be covered by the latest accepted test gate or explicit verificationEvidence.
Source test intent must be represented by target tests, manual/emulator evidence, or a documented harness blocker.
Replace root/shell/whole-file endpoints with precise target functions, types, resource files, or tests before closure.
architectureHints: Do not close this group only with a root view, whole-file, state bucket, or shell endpoint.
If several source files are merged, keep business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints.

implementationGroups.title: Verification and evidence policy
requiredBehaviors: Verify the phase's implemented behavior with focused tests, manual/emulator evidence, or explicit verificationEvidence.

build pass alone does not close per-feature behavior
manual review evidence must point to concrete target endpoints
acceptanceEvidence: Update source-coverage-matrix.json after implementation with targetEndpoints, tests, parityLevel, and verificationEvidence.
If tests cannot run, record the concrete harness blocker instead of claiming test PASS.

repairQueue.purpose: Full grouped repair queue for the translation agent. The queue indexes likely-related problems and evidence; each group still requires source/target readback, build/test checks, and authoritative matrix/capability updates before it can be considered repaired.
sweepGuidance: Process repair clusters in order and try to make progress on every listed cluster.
Within each cluster, sweep the referenced child groups instead of stopping after the first successful fix.
For each child group, verify source behavior and current target implementation before changing code or records.
When prep-contract groups are present, satisfy them as phase-level contracts before treating row-level findings as closed.
Implement real behavior or platform replacement when feasible; otherwise improve evidence or record a concrete blocker.
Do not stop after the first repaired group, and do not hard-close platform blockers without pure Cangjie evidence or a target-context probe.

clusters.title: Whole-project findings: implement-or-structure
clusters.suggestedHandling: Treat this as the prompt-facing parent cluster. Sweep every child group listed in groupIds; use the child group items in translation-quality-summary.json for exact feature, capability, source path, endpoint, and evidence details.
clusters.blockerPolicy: If a child group is blocked by platform/product/tooling limits, record that blocker precisely and continue to the next child group instead of stopping the whole cluster.
